subject: Product Management
title: Quibi
description: A micro-lesson that makes Quibi usable.

hook.text: Quibi raised $1.75 billion to build 'quick bites' — premium short-form video for mobile. It shut down in six months.

buildup.text: Jeffrey Katzenberg and Meg Whitman believed people wanted Hollywood-quality shows in 10-minute episodes for their commute.

discovery.text: The assumption was wrong. People already had short-form content — it was free, on YouTube and TikTok.

twist.text: Quibi launched in April 2020 — during COVID lockdowns. Nobody was commuting. The core use case evaporated on day one.

climax.text: Quibi proved that money can't buy product-market fit. $1.75 billion in funding couldn't overcome a flawed value proposition.

punchline.text: No amount of funding can fix a product nobody needs.

quiz.question: Why did Quibi fail despite massive funding?
options: The value proposition was flawed — free short-form content already existed
The technology platform had too many bugs
It didn't spend enough on marketing
